title: Pair 04: No Reservation Hold vs Reservation Hold
summary: This comparison keeps the fine-dining room, a reservation-heavy evening with late arrivals and no-shows, and service rules fixed, but changes how strongly bookings are protected. Option A releases tables immediately if the booked party has not arrived, while Option B protects those tables through the grace period to show the trade-off between honoring reservations and keeping walk-ins moving.

A.label: No hold after booking time
A.notes: Tables return to the floor immediately if the booked party has not yet arrived.

B.label: Protect bookings through grace period
B.notes: Tables remain protected for booked guests while the grace window is still open.
